title: New Year's Day
description: Welcome the upcoming year with joy, reflection, and renewed academic goals! The entire school campus will remain closed as we celebrate a fresh start full of bright educational milestones, personal development milestones, and prospective institution-wide successes. Students are encouraged to utilize this time to draft their personal growth trackers and prepare mentally for an intense, rewarding upcoming semester.

title: School Reopens
description: Regular academic classes resume promptly after the rejuvenating winter vacation break. All students are expected to report in proper, neat winter uniforms with completed holiday homework dossiers and project assignments prepared for formal evaluation. Class mentors will conduct uniform checks and host an introductory goal-setting session during the initial homeroom period to streamline the post-break transition.

title: Inter-House Cricket Selection
description: Unleash your ultimate athletic potential at the formal selection trials for both senior and junior school cricket teams. Participants must bring their individual personal sports kits; mandatory registration begins at the main pavilion ground sharp at 2:30 PM. Sports department selectors will evaluate candidates based on bowling accuracy, batting technique, fielding agility, and overall sportsmanship metrics during live net sessions.

title: Republic Day
description: Commemoration of Republic Day featuring a disciplined ceremonial flag hoisting, synchronized march pasts by the student council scouts, and patriotic inter-house group song performances across the central school grounds. Attendance is mandatory for all students. Parents are cordially invited to witness the parade and the subsequent distribution of national bravery student awards by our honorable chief guest.

title: Pre-Board Exams Begin
description: The final cycle of preparatory standard evaluations commences for all graduating board classes. These formal mock examinations are designed to strictly simulate board conditions, allowing teachers to evaluate curriculum readiness and time management metrics. Late entries will not be entertained under any circumstances, and students must bring transparent pencil pouches containing only verified writing materials.

title: Maha Shivratri
description: The institution will observe a full closed non-working campus day in observance of the auspicious festival of Maha Shivratri. Regular administrative operations, ongoing remedial coaching, and standard classroom lectures will remain suspended throughout the day. The hostel blocks will arrange a special traditional satvik menu for residential students observing fasts during this festive occasion.

title: Holi Festival
description: Celebrate the dynamic festival of colors with your classmates and instructors! The management is hosting a community gathering featuring safe organic herbal colors, authentic traditional sweets, and cultural musical acts, followed by the standard institutional holiday. To ensure safety, students are strictly prohibited from bringing synthetic color packets, water balloons, or chemical dyes into the school perimeter.

title: Annual Final Examinations
description: Year-end final session assessments begin for all non-board academic standards. Students are advised to double-check their specific seating arrangements and carry valid institutional identity cards alongside permitted examination stationery items. Digital smartwatches, smartphones, programmable calculators, and unauthorized sheets of paper are completely prohibited inside the examination halls to maintain transparency.

title: Eid al-Fitr
description: In joyful celebration marking the conclusion of the holy month of Ramadan fasting, the school campus will remain closed for Eid al-Fitr. We wish our entire school family, students, parents, and supporting staff a rewarding day filled with peace and harmony. Regular online portal access and administrative query desks will remain offline during this period to let our staff celebrate with family.

title: Report Card Day
description: Declaration of annual academic results along with mandatory parent-teacher interactive consultations. Parents will receive comprehensive grade logs, progress analytics, and behavior reports while collaborating directly with class mentors to chart path plans. Students must accompany their parents wearing their complete official school uniform to collect their final promotions dossiers.

title: Good Friday
description: The institution will observe an official holiday on the solemn occasion of Good Friday. All standard learning schedules, interactive virtual portals, administrative desks, and supplementary co-curricular clubs will be paused for the duration of the holiday. Normal routines and scheduling functions will resume automatically on the next working morning.

title: New Academic Session Begins
description: Welcome back to school! Commencement of classroom instruction for the new academic terms. Orientation protocols will be executed by house masters, textbook distribution schedules will be finalized, and student groups will be introduced to updated syllabus trackers. Please ensure that all custom notebooks, text resources, and laboratory manuals are procured from the stationary counter prior to your morning classes.

title: Inter-School Football Tournament
description: The highly anticipated opening competitive clash of the regional inter-school premier football league. Come out to cheer for our elite varsity squad as they defend the championship trophy on home turf against talented visiting regional teams. The student council will set up refreshing cheering zones around the perimeter fields, and light refreshments will be accessible at the sports arena stalls.

title: Summer Vacation Begins
description: The official closure of the primary term as school breaks for the long-awaited annual summer holiday sequence. Students will be provided customized assignments, interactive project guides, and curated reading lists to maintain academic engagement over the break. The physical campus will handle limited operations, restricted strictly to necessary admission tasks and critical documentation requests.

title: Eid al-Adha (Bakrid)
description: In religious observance of Eid al-Adha, all operations within the school campus stand suspended. We hope this festive period serves as an enriching time of charity, deep spiritual reflection, and unified family celebrations for everyone within our local community. Regular classroom frameworks and custom tracking modules will remain paused during this designated period.

title: School Reopens after Summer Break
description: Regular attendance tracks and core academic timelines resume instantly following the conclusion of the summer break. All departments will resume daily operations, and students must turn in completed holiday portfolios to their subject teachers by the end of the week. Defaulters will face strict internal score deductions in their continuous internal valuation metrics.

title: Periodic Test I Begins
description: The primary cycle of short-form unit evaluations commences systematically across all classes. These targeted unit tests measure comprehensive grasp of post-vacation learning structures and will influence final cumulative internal assessment metrics. The exact room wise mapping and physical test roll charts will be displayed across major floor bulletin boards.

title: Monsoon Swimming Gala
description: Dive into competitive fun at our annual aquatic meet! The timeline features thrilling freestyle dashes, butterfly sprints, and inter-house relay matches inside the indoor swimming facility. Ensure medical fitness clearance certificates are submitted ahead of time. Spectators must remain inside the designated upper viewing gallery deck to ensure smooth competition flow.

title: Independence Day
description: Join us in celebrating our nation's historic milestone at the 80th Independence Day assembly. The schedule highlights national flag unfurling, honors addresses by the director, and expressive thematic dance dramas performed by senior fine arts branches. A sweet distribution counter will follow the main assembly, and tracking for uniform point systems will be stringently active.

title: Raksha Bandhan
description: The school will remain closed to allow students, faculty, and administrative support groups to enjoy the traditional festivities of Raksha Bandhan. We wish everyone a wonderful day celebrating the beautiful protective bonds shared among siblings. All co-curricular activities, including sports practices and lab experiments, stand deferred.

title: Janmashtami
description: In observance of Krishna Janmashtami, all regular classroom lectures and tests are officially deferred. The school community is encouraged to safely participate in festive rituals reflecting on messages of righteousness and ethical duty. Normal schedules will operate properly on the subsequent operational calendar layout.

title: Teachers Day Special Assembly
description: A dynamic student-led cultural tribute layout dedicated to honoring our hard-working faculty members. The student council will manage special entertainment segments, personalized appreciation badges, and musical performances to express deep gratitude. Regular academic textbooks can be left behind as class structures transition into creative brainstorming zones after lunch.

title: Annual Badminton Championship
description: The singles and doubles knockout competition stages arrive at the indoor sports complex arena. Top-seeded players from all school houses will battle under standard championship conditions. Equipment inspection starts at the registration desks. Ensure you are wearing non-marking sports shoes to preserve court surface integrity.

title: Gandhi Jayanti
description: Commemorating the birth anniversary of Mahatma Gandhi through targeted community service initiatives. Students will participate in localized Swachh Bharat cleanliness runs, peaceful plantation actions, and essay writing forums regarding non-violence. Participation records will feed directly into seasonal internal social work portfolios.

title: Dussehra Celebration
description: Socio-cultural presentations celebrating Vijayadashami to mark the ultimate triumph of moral righteousness over corruption. The schedule features a traditional dynamic Ramlila theatrical adaptation, followed by the declaration of the official festival holiday break. Students are asked to review their homework repositories on the cloud portal before leaving.

title: Diwali Vacation
description: The auspicious Diwali festive holiday break begins for all blocks. We wish our students a secure, green, and luminous festival of lights. Remember to safely complete your designated autumn assignment workbooks during the holidays. The institutional physical campus will handle minimal administrative inquiries on pre-planned emergency timelines.

title: Inter-House Basketball League
description: The final high-stakes basketball matches to decide the champion house pool for the current session. Come out and energetically support your players as they display exceptional defensive maneuvers and team synergy on the court. Refreshment tokens can be picked up near the main storage lockup entry point.

title: Guru Nanak Jayanti
description: The school will remain closed in profound observance of Guru Nanak Gurpurab. Let us utilize this sacred occasion to appreciate his universal teachings of selfless community service, absolute equality, and peaceful coexistence across humankind. Standard tracking modules will pause until normal operational layouts execute.

title: Mid-term Exams begin
venue: Centralized Test Blocks A, B & C
description: Mid-term examinations start for all primary, secondary, and higher-secondary blocks. Ensure you arrive at least 30 minutes before the scheduled distribution of answer scripts, carrying valid geometry equipment and writing accessories. Any student caught with written notes or contraband items will be permanently debarred from evaluations.

title: Sports Day
description: The grand annual school athletic meet highlights competitive track dashes, high jump qualifiers, shot put throws, and mass drill formations. Parents are cordially invited to witness the prize distribution ceremony from the main stadium balcony. Competitors must dress inside their certified house tracks and check in at the field stalls.

title: Annual Day Celebration
description: Our premier Annual Day cultural festival takes center stage inside the grand main auditorium. Experience a night of artistic excellence with full orchestral symphonies, historical dramas, and special merit recognition honors across fields. Attendees are requested to take their assigned cluster seats by 4:45 PM to guarantee a smooth live transmission broadcast setup.

title: Christmas holiday
description: The institution will observe a winter break for Christmas. All instructional layouts, dynamic labs, and administrative operations will remain closed as families gather together to celebrate this festive winter occasion with joy. Special decorative displays arranged by the fine arts branch will remain illuminated in the front entrance rotunda.

title: Parent Teacher Meeting
description: The mid-term review parent-teacher meeting provides a dedicated space to examine exam answer keys, review learning curves, and outline corrective actions for the upcoming session. Full attendance is highly encouraged. Detailed student analytics files and tailored remedial learning guides will be handed over by core branch advisors.

title: Holiday Day
description: A special secondary athletic showcase day tailored for junior school divisions to demonstrate physical coordination, teamwork, and athletic skills via fun obstacle courses, simple relays, and community-building games on the open field lawns. Participation medallions will be distributed to every candidate at the final assembly point.
